arts.ttl: PSG v Aston Villa: Champions League quarter-final, first leg – live
arts.sum: Kick-off at Parc des Princes is 8pm (BST) Follow Barcelona v Dortmund live Six years ago tomorrow, ten-man Aston Villa came from behind to win 2-1 at Rotherham and move up to fifth in the Championship. Jack Grealish scored the winner, Tyrone Mings was sent off and, in the words of the Villa manager Dean Smith, “John McGinn was so good I thought there were two of him”. In the same week, Thomas Tuchel’s Paris Saint-Germain moved 20 points clear at the top of Ligue 1, with a game in hand. Mings, McGinn and millions of Villa fans have had a helluva ride since then, particularly since Unai Emery came to the club in 2022, and tonight they will play PSG in the last eight of Europe’s greatest competition. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Champions League suspension rules explained as Aston Villa face four bans vs PSG
arts.sum: Aston Villa and Paris Saint-Germain will be wary of any yellow cards specific players receive during their Champions League quarter-final due to the possibility of second-leg suspensions. arts.ttl: Unai Emery offers explanation for Marcus Rashford improvement
arts.sum: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery has put Marcus Rashford's return to form since joining the club on loan down to the player "smiling" and enjoying football again.
